## [00:00 - 03:01]

Let's talk about how to set up lead scoring automation in infusion soft. You'll then be able to leverage that for your email marketing and meta advertising. We're just going to scratch the surface here. You can go much greater detail. If you check out my blog post at johnlumer.com slash scoring. So the primary point here is we're trying to isolate our most engaged leads as part of our email list. So we can then leverage that for whatever reason that you want. For my purpose, I want to focus on those people who are clicking links that I'm sending them in order to maximize the number of emails I'm sending to the people who actually want to get them and click. Again, you can leverage that for your meta advertising as well. I actually struggled for years trying to find a way to set up something like this, but there is a way to do it. And I'm going to walk through that today. It involves tags and an automation campaign and lead scoring set up. Even if you don't use infusion soft, maybe this can inspire some ideas that you can apply to whatever your CRM is. So again, my focus is on people who have clicked links recently so I can email them more links again, not to overdo it. But if they're not clicking anymore, I won't email them as often. So the system I have set up will give them a certain score based on how recently they clicked on a link in an email. So I've got five flames. If it happened during the last seven days, four flames, three flames, two flames all the way down to one flame. This goes up out to 42 days for two flames. And if you clicked on any link prior to that, you'll just retain the one flame. But if you never click on any link ever, then you'll just get no flames, which means I may rarely email you at that point. I just started this system in February at the very beginning of the month and the early returns are really good. But I got to repeat that this is early returns. My click rate is at 3.7% right now, which is up from 2.4% in 2023. But some of these emails get 10 to 20% even higher click through rate, depending on if I'm targeting just those people are most engaged. But my opt out rate is remaining very low. It's at 0.06%, which is the lowest it's ever been. It was 0.07% in 2023, but that was actually the best I've ever had for a given year. Of course, it is a balance because if all we cared about was click rate and opt out rate, we just wouldn't send very many emails. So we want to kind of push that lever a little bit to get to a point where maybe people are starting to opt out a little bit more. I want to drive more traffic to my website because this is one of the few places you have a little bit more control over that instead of relying on organic Google search or organic social media or Facebook ads as an example.

## [03:01 - 06:03]

So the first thing you need to do is set up tags. I'm not going to go through that process right now. Just know that these are kind of what you need. You can name them whatever you want. You need a start that starts the campaign. And this happens when people click on a link, if you're going to follow all of my steps. Active clicks start. And then I have one tag for each of the various levels. I have five different levels as part of my lead scoring. If you have fewer than that, you don't need five. And just know I manually add this tag to any important link that is in one of my emails. So active clicks start. So if they click on that link, it will automatically send them into this campaign. Next, we need to create the automation. It seems very basic on the surface. It's going to look a little bit more complicated when you dig in. But let's get to it. So first, we need a tag that starts the whole process. That was when we were just talking about active clicks start. Once they get that tag, they're sent into this sequence. One to five flame sequence is what I called it. Again, this looks complicated. It's not that complicated. Each of these steps are kind of similar. So the first thing it's going to do is apply this tag to get five flames. Because they just clicked a link. It's within the first seven days. We want to make sure that they are five flames. We also want to remove five tags. So first of all, the active clicks that needs to be removed. Because if they click again, we want them to start over. And if they had any of these tags already, because they were at that level, we need to remove that because it doesn't matter anymore that they were at one flame or two flames. If they clicked recently, they should be at five. Then we're going to run a timer for seven days, because that's the level they should maintain for the next seven days. Again, use whatever you want there for a timer. Then if they haven't clicked again within seven days, we are going to give them the four flames tag and we'll remove the five flames tag. And again, I'm going to start a seven day timer. So if they don't click on anything again for another seven days, they're going to go to three flames. So we'll add that tag for three flames, remove four flames. And then I'm starting a timer for 14 days at this point. Do whatever you want. Nothing crazy for the next step. We're going to go into add the two flames tag. They still haven't clicked and we're going to remove the three flames tag and another 14 day timer. And then finally, if they still haven't clicked again, they'll be moved down to one flame and then we're going to remove the two flames tag. I don't have a timer here because I feel if you've clicked at any point and you just never click again, you'll just retain the one flame level. And then the final step is we add this start tag again at the very end to restart them, assuming they click again. Now, finally, we need to set up our lead scoring. So go to your main menu under CRM, go to settings.

## [06:04 - 08:19]

You'll find scores. I have five points equaling five flames. Do whatever you want. Then we need to create rules. So you can create rules based on a context activity or tags. Activities are a little bit too basic for me. That's why I went this route. So I'm doing it by tag. It's all pretty simple. If the contacts tags contains this specific tag for five flames, they get five points. It never expires because that tag will be removed automatically. Same thing if they have the tag for four flames, three flames, two and one. So it's all really simple. The primary thing I do with that is control who gets my emails. I create save lists based on how many flames that you have at the time. So I can email those people a little bit more often than everybody else. So how big that audience is depends a lot on what exactly it is I'm promoting and maybe what the frequency of my broadcast has been. Of course, this is also something you can leverage with your meta advertising. This is something I am experimenting with right now, because anyone who's followed my stuff knows I have struggled for years trying to find the best ways to drive quality traffic to my blog posts with ads. It's not easy. We're not going to go into all that right now. So this is something I've found is actually a little bit more effective than another approach I've used, which is optimizing for custom events based on things like time spent, scroll depth and other things. The issue is going to be that these audiences are very small or can be, depending on the size of your list and creative fatigue can be a quick issue. So I'm rotating ads in, which would be promoting different blog posts every three or four days or so. But the results have been really good.
